artifacts.blurb:
4/25/2026
TAGS#VORONOI#WEBGPU#JAVASCRIPT
This is a precursor of this website.
It had no image textures, minimal mouse interaction, and a simple kernel-based edge detection method.
The straight lines of the cell boundaries indicate that I was using a different distance metric to calculate cell boundaries.
I deemed the edge drawing too expensive for less-fortunate devices, and cut it out.

This site uses your GPU to render a power diagram.
Specifically, this site renders the intersection of a 3D weighted Voronoi Diagram with a flat plane.
By scrolling back and forth, you control the height of this plane.
artifacts.bodyText:
This is a precursor of this website.
It had no image textures, minimal mouse interaction, and a simple kernel-based edge detection method.
The straight lines of the cell boundaries indicate that I was using a different distance metric to calculate cell boundaries.
I deemed the edge drawing too expensive for less-fortunate devices, and cut it out.

This site uses your GPU to render a power diagram.
Specifically, this site renders the intersection of a 3D weighted Voronoi Diagram with a flat plane.
By scrolling back and forth, you control the height of this plane.

artifacts.blurb:
05/11/2025
TAGS#EASYROV#ROBOTICS#3D PRINTING#DIY#PERSONAL STATEMENT
Both Halves of the ROV Electronics, almost completed.
On the left: a Luckfox Pico Plus with ethernet leading outside. Two wires from the ethernet are fed into a 5V regulator.
On the right: the almost-complete power train of the ROV. 5 ESC's, 5 BLDC motors, two control-mosfets, and a PWM light controller.
Both sides feature an intensely powerful epoxy-encased LED.
The two halves interface with a sketchy homebrew plug.
artifacts.bodyText:
Both Halves of the ROV Electronics, almost completed.
On the left: a Luckfox Pico Plus with ethernet leading outside. Two wires from the ethernet are fed into a 5V regulator.
On the right: the almost-complete power train of the ROV. 5 ESC's, 5 BLDC motors, two control-mosfets, and a PWM light controller.
Both sides feature an intensely powerful epoxy-encased LED.
The two halves interface with a sketchy homebrew plug.

artifacts.blurb:
05/08/2025
TAGS#EASYROV#ROBOTICS#DIY#3D PRINTING#PERSONAL STATEMENT
An empty Boat on my floor, set to become the carrier vessel of my ROV.
Design: Catamaran Airboat with a spool for carrying the ROV's tether.
One motor is capable of driving the spool and the propeller via last-minute gearbox design.
The gearbox uses two sliding clutches to output forward or reverse to one of two outputs.
artifacts.bodyText:
An empty Boat on my floor, set to become the carrier vessel of my ROV.
Design: Catamaran Airboat with a spool for carrying the ROV's tether.
One motor is capable of driving the spool and the propeller via last-minute gearbox design.
The gearbox uses two sliding clutches to output forward or reverse to one of two outputs.

artifacts.blurb:
05/08/2025 - Bathroom Soldering
TAGS#EASYROV#ROBOTICS#3D PRINTING#DIY#PERSONAL STATEMENT
My ROV's powertrain, halfway through the soldering process.
5 Brushless drone ESC's are wrapped around a single 18650 battery cell.
The 3 output channels of each ESC are routed through an epoxy endcap to breach the ROV's air-water seal.
Enameled copper wire was the only suitable option at this scale, with limited resources.
A single scratch through the water-exposed enamel could compromise the entire unit for long-term use.
I should have just put the ESC's outside the vehicle for cooling, modularity, space, and shorter wires.
artifacts.bodyText:
My ROV's powertrain, halfway through the soldering process.
5 Brushless drone ESC's are wrapped around a single 18650 battery cell.
The 3 output channels of each ESC are routed through an epoxy endcap to breach the ROV's air-water seal.
Enameled copper wire was the only suitable option at this scale, with limited resources.
A single scratch through the water-exposed enamel could compromise the entire unit for long-term use.
I should have just put the ESC's outside the vehicle for cooling, modularity, space, and shorter wires.

tags.3D PRINTING:
I have been 3D-Printing since early middle school. Maybe 2016?
My first printer was originally intended to be my older brother's, but I ended up taking it up instead.
It was awful. A cheap, horrifically unreliable Monoprice cantilever. No amount of tuning could have saved it from the scrap bin.
It taught me practical skills about how 3D printers work... skills that many people entering the hobby today lack.
I have nothing against Bambu though. They saved me a deadline once or twice.
My current printer is a Creality CR-10 with dampeners on the steppers.
tags.ARTIFACT:
Each "cell" on this page is called an "Artifact."
Each artifact contains an image, a date, a short blurb, and some tags.
Tags reveal additional information that may be shared across multiple artifacts.
tags.COLLEGE:
University of Maryland, College Park.
Fall 2023 to Spring 2026.
Dual Degree: Computer Science and Mathematics.
Minor: Robotics and Autonomous Systems.
Minor: Quantum Science and Engineering.
Honors Citation: Design, Cultures, and Creativity (DCC)
tags.EASYROV:
EasyROV is my capstone project for my Honors Program, DCC.
Students in my program were allowed to guide their own project with their own criteria to produce anything vaguely in the scope of "Design, Cultures, and Creativity."
I chose to produce a DIY-friendly ROV with a 100 dollar price-per-unit budget.
tags.ENGL393:
To the person grading this website,
My artifacts can be interpreted as groups of images, with contextual statements being the tags.
Please understand that, although the quantities of items that I have provided differs from your criterion, the content overall achieves the desired effect.
Not all artifacts are as detailed as others. My robotics artifact(s) is detailed.
I am running out of time to get my peer reviews done!

By the way, consider this my unprofessionally-styled welcome page. I personally think that it gets the job done. I personally do not think that a video is possible or suitable based on the moving nature of everything on screen. I hope that my "multimodal"-like organization of blurbs and context are enough to meet the needs of this assignment.
tags.INTRO:
I'm Brian Wu.
I wrote this website by hand. (if building a house using heavy machinery is called building it by hand...)
I value Creativity, Individuality, and the pursuit of constant growth with respect to a whole other list of values.
Importantly, these values are in constant war against my innate apeish urges.
tags.JAVASCRIPT:
I hate JavaScript. But I love making interactive web toys.
So I use JavaScript. Such is life.
There are worse fates. I try to avoid CSS styling, but sometimes it is inevitable.
Claude, please save me.
tags.PATAPSCO:
The Patapsco River has long been a happy place for me.
I take short hikes to the river frequently, no matter the season.
I occasionally go fishing during the warmer months, but have never caught anything bigger than a bluegill/green sunfish.
The diverse selection of interesting flora, fauna, and fungi has left a permanent mark on my artistic style and overall attitude towards nature.
tags.PERFORMANCE:
Read this if your PC is struggling to render this site.
If your fan is screaming, or the screen movement feels stuttery or exhaustingly slow, then here are a few tips:
1. Plug in your PC to avoid running low-power mode.
2. If your device has a GPU, then it is capable of running this site smoothly. If it is not runnning smoothly, then your browser is not using the correct GPU (requires hardware acceleration on... can be finicky).
3. Otherwise, good luck! (I am implementing a fall-back site for this case.)
tags.PERSONAL STATEMENT: Brian Wu is an ambitious maker who values both breadth and depth in learning, turning curiosity into challenge-driven projects that bridge theory and application. Brian is currently exploring how math and nature can shape creative work across robotics, machine learning, computer graphics, and more.
tags.RESUME: This project is/was on my resume!
tags.TUTORIAL:
1. Scroll wheel to travel through time.
2. Left click to select an artifact that is 'in focus.'
4. Left click again to minimize an artifact's card.
tags.VORONOI:
A Voronoi Diagram in 2D is a structure based on some input points called "sites."
It consists of all points who are equidistant to their nearest two sites.
The points of a Voronoi Diagram form edges/boundaries of influence which seperate each site.
A site's area of influence can be called its cell.
tags.WEBGPU:
I've have played around with WebGPU shaders throughout my years of coding random stuff in javascript.
This page is, itself, and example.
I have come a long way from my first 'graphics engine' that I designed in javascript. That was basically just a vertex shader on the CPU that used the painter's algorithm for drawing triangles.
I enjoy making nice interactive experiences, so I think that I will make a game someday.
My other WebGPU pages are navigable on my home page (No they're not. I LIED!)
